name: shader-clippy
description: Linter for HLSL and Slang — performance + correctness rules beyond what dxc catches. End-to-end coverage of both languages (AST + CFG + reflection) via tree-sitter-hlsl + tree-sitter-slang.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Shader Clippy
walkthroughs.description: Squiggle-by-squiggle introduction to the HLSL + Slang linter.

steps.title: Open an HLSL or Slang file
steps.description:
Open any `.hlsl`, `.hlsli`, `.fx`, `.fxh`, `.vsh`, `.psh`, `.csh`, `.gsh`, `.hsh`, `.dsh`, or `.slang` file in your workspace. The extension activates automatically when one is open.

**`.slang` reflection-only baseline (v1.3, ADR 0020 sub-phase A):** ~32 of 189 rules fire on Slang sources today (resource-binding, cbuffer-padding, descriptor-table, format-mismatch). AST + control-flow rules wait on `tree-sitter-slang` integration in v1.4+. Set `shaderClippy.slang.enable=false` to defer to the official `shader-slang.slang` extension exclusively.

If you don't have one handy, [create a new untitled file](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile) and set its language to HLSL or Slang via the bottom-right language indicator.

steps.title: Check the status bar
steps.description:
Look at the bottom-right of the VS Code window. The Shader Clippy status badge tells you whether the LSP server is running:

- $(check) Shader Clippy → ready, will lint on every edit
- $(sync~spin) Shader Clippy → starting up
- $(error) Shader Clippy → failed to start; click the badge to see why

[Show the output channel](command:shaderClippy.showOutput) for the full activation log.

steps.title: Trigger a rule
steps.description:
Type a known-bad pattern into your HLSL file to see the linter fire. For example:

```hlsl
float squared(float x) { return pow(x, 2.0); }
```

A squiggle should appear under `pow(x, 2.0)` and the diagnostic lands in the [Problems panel](command:workbench.actions.view.problems) (`Ctrl+Shift+M` / `Cmd+Shift+M`).

steps.title: Apply a quick-fix
steps.description:
Hover over a squiggle and click the lightbulb (or press `Ctrl+.` / `Cmd+.`) to see machine-applicable fixes. For `pow-const-squared` the fix rewrites `pow(x, 2.0)` to `x * x` automatically.

Not every rule has a quick-fix; rules tagged `applicability: suggestion` show a description without an automatic edit.

steps.title: Configure the linter
steps.description:
Drop a `.shader-clippy.toml` next to your shaders to override severity per rule, exclude paths, or pin a target Slang profile. Example:

```toml
[rules]
"pow-const-squared" = "deny"
"redundant-saturate" = "warn"
"min16float-in-cbuffer-roundtrip" = "allow"
```

Full schema: [Configuration docs](https://github.com/NelCit/shader-clippy/blob/main/docs/configuration.md). Run [Shader Clippy: Re-lint Active Document](command:shaderClippy.relintDocument) after edits to apply changes immediately.

shaderClippy.serverPath.description: Explicit path to shader-clippy-lsp binary (overrides auto-discovery).

shaderClippy.targetProfile.description: Slang target profile (e.g., sm_6_6, vs_6_7, ps_6_8). Empty = server default.

shaderClippy.includeDirectories.description: Additional shader include directories passed to Slang reflection. Supports ${workspaceFolder}.

shaderClippy.enableReflection.description: Enable Phase 3 reflection-aware rules (requires Slang compile).

shaderClippy.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VS Code and the shader-clippy LSP server.

enumDescriptions: No inline message; rely on squiggles + Problems panel.
Show inline message at end of line for Error severity only.
Show inline message at end of line for every severity (Error + Warning + Info).
shaderClippy.inlineDiagnostics.description: Render the diagnostic message at the end of the offending line (Error Lens style).

shaderClippy.showStatusBar.description: Show the Shader Clippy badge in the status bar (severity-split diagnostic count + click for actions). Disable if your status bar is crowded.

shaderClippy.slang.enable.description: Recognise `.slang` files (ADR 0020 sub-phase A — v1.3+). When `false`, the extension does not handle `.slang` documents — useful when you rely on the official `shader-slang.slang` extension exclusively. Note: `.slang` source-language support is reflection-only on v1.3 (~32 of 189 rules fire); full AST + CFG coverage tracks for v1.4+.
